title: Buxus pedicellata
taxonomy: Buxus pedicellata is classified as a species of plant in the family Buxaceae. It belongs to the genus Buxus, which includes other boxwood species. The family Buxaceae is a small group of flowering plants commonly known as the boxwoods. Buxus pedicellata is native to tropical regions and is often used in landscaping due to its compact growth habit. The exact classification of Buxus pedicellata may vary depending on the source, but it is generally accepted as a species within the Buxaceae family.
uses: Buxus pedicellata is a species of box tree that has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. In traditional medicine, the leaves of Buxus pedicellata are used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant is also believed to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Buxus pedicellata.

side_effects: Buxus pedicellata, a plant native to tropical Africa, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and like many plants in the Buxus genus, it may have side effects when used improperly or in large quantities.
